mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a combined signal accuracy below 50% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports equity valuations and yields positive 20-day forward returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of expansion-phase observations, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation indexed by CPIAUCSL breaches 225.0, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 225.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with declining retail sales signals demand destruction, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and RSAFS declines coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in over 65% of late-cycle observations, invalidating the demand destruction channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive equity market sentiment within five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show no predictive power for 5-day SPX direction, with a hit rate below 50% across all market conditions.
